courseleaf.title: Social, Ethnic & Gender Relations, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
The interdisciplinary minor in Social, Ethnic & Gender Relations explores the racial, ethnic, class, and gender aspects of human relations in the modern world. Students study human societies and cultures from a multi-ethnic perspective and across established academic departmental lines. The minor is jointly sponsored by African American & African Studies, American Studies, Asian American Studies, Chicana/o Studies, Native American Studies, and Gender, Sexuality & Women’s Studies.

Advising

Katherine Ampaw-Matthei; Jeremiah Thompson
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
Choose one upper division course from five of the following six groups to total 20 units:20
(1) African American & African Studies (AAS).
(2) Asian American Studies (ASA).
(3) Chicana/o Studies (CHI).
(4) Gender, Sexuality & Women's Studies.
(5) Native American Studies (NAS).
(6) American Studies (AMS); with Domestic Diversity GE designation.
Restriction
No more than one course applied toward the satisfaction of a major may be offered in satisfaction of the minor.
Total Units20
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The interdisciplinary Social, Ethnic & Gender Relations minor explores the racial, ethnic, class, and gender aspects of human relations in the modern world. Students study human societies and cultures from a multi-ethnic perspective and across established academic departmental lines.
